title: gahnia vitiensis
intro: Gahnia vitiensis, also known as Fiji saw-sedge or pōhutukawa, is a type of grass-like plant native to the Pacific Islands and Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Gahnia vitiensis include treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a wound healing agent. The herbal preparations of Gahnia vitiensis typically involve drying the plant material, then using it in various forms, including teas, infusions, and topical applications.
